q.en: At the start of the book, who is Mario?
q.zh: 在书的一开头，马里奥是谁？

options.en: A scientist studying nutrition at the USDA according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一位在农业部研究营养的科学家（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A farmer who grows surplus corn
options.zh: 一位种植多余玉米的农民

options.en: The mayor of a large city
A boy whose family runs out of food money by the end of each month
options.zh: 一个家里每到月底就没钱买食物的男孩

explain.en: Mario's mom works two jobs, but by the end of the month they always run out of money for food, so his only meal that day was the school lunch.
explain.zh: 马里奥的妈妈打两份工，但每到月底家里总没钱买食物，所以他那天唯一的食物就是学校午餐。

q.en: According to 2014 USDA numbers, about how many American children lived in food-insecure homes?
q.zh: 根据美国农业部2014年的数据，大约有多少美国儿童生活在食物无保障的家庭？

options.en: About 15.3 million
About 1.5 million
About 60 million
About 48.1 million (all Americans)

explain.en: The book states 48.1 million Americans were food insecure, including 15.3 million children, about one in five children.
explain.zh: 书中说4810万美国人食物无保障，其中包括1530万儿童，约五分之一的儿童。

q.en: What did the CDC find about children who do not get enough to eat?
q.zh: 美国疾控中心对吃不饱的孩子有什么发现？

options.en: They grow taller than average
They learn faster than other children according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他们比其他孩子学得更快（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: They eat more at school
They have more health problems and miss more school
options.zh: 他们健康问题更多，缺课也更多

explain.en: The CDC found hungry children have more health problems, miss more school, and have a harder time learning.
explain.zh: 疾控中心发现饥饿儿童健康问题更多、缺课更多，学习也更困难。

q.en: Why does the book point out that most food-assistance recipients are women?
q.zh: 书里为什么特别指出大多数领取食物援助的是女性？

options.en: Women do not like to work
Single mothers are especially likely to live in poverty
options.zh: 单亲妈妈尤其容易陷入贫困

options.en: Women eat less food
Men cannot receive assistance according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 男性不能领取援助（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: The book notes over 60 percent of adults receiving food assistance are women and single mothers are twice as likely as single fathers to live in poverty.
explain.zh: 书中提到领取食物援助的成年人中超过60%是女性，单亲妈妈陷入贫困的概率是单亲爸爸的两倍。

q.en: What does the term 'food desert' mean in this book?
q.zh: 在本书中，“食物荒漠”指的是什么？

options.en: A dry climate with almost no rain according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 几乎不下雨的干旱气候（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: A place with no large grocery stores, often in high-poverty areas
options.zh: 没有大型杂货店的地方，往往位于高贫困区

options.en: A type of desert plant
A government food program

explain.en: Food deserts are high-poverty areas not served by large grocery stores, where people cannot easily travel to buy nutritious food.
explain.zh: 食物荒漠是指高贫困地区没有大型杂货店，居民难以买到有营养的食物。

q.en: In what year was the Food Stamp Program renamed SNAP?
q.zh: “食品券计划”是在哪一年改名为SNAP的？

options.en: 1933 according to some versions of the story

explain.en: In 2008 the Food Stamp Program was renamed the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) to stress its focus on nutrition.
explain.zh: 2008年，“食品券计划”改名为“补充营养援助计划”（SNAP），以强调其对营养的重视。

q.en: Which child's project brought in the largest amount of food?
q.zh: 哪个孩子的项目募集到的食物最多？

options.en: William Winslow, who collected 8,100 pounds of food
options.zh: 威廉·温斯洛收集了8100磅食物

options.en: Sam Adamo, who raised $700 playing cello
options.zh: 萨姆·亚当用大提琴演奏筹得700美元

options.en: Macy Stewart, who collected 844 pounds of food
options.zh: 梅西·斯图尔特收集了844磅食物

options.en: Carson Pazdan, who made a cookbook raising over $20,000
options.zh: 卡森·帕兹丹编食谱筹得逾2万美元

explain.en: William Winslow collected 8,100 pounds of food for BackPack Buddies in 2016, more than the others listed.
explain.zh: 威廉·温斯洛在2016年为“背包伙伴”募集了8100磅食物，比书中其他孩子都多。

q.en: Why does the author include stories of what specific children accomplished?
q.zh: 作者为什么收录这些孩子具体事迹的故事？

options.en: To inspire readers that any kid can make a difference
options.zh: 为了激励读者：任何孩子都能带来改变

options.en: To show that only adults can solve hunger according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了说明只有成年人能解决饥饿（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To explain how to cook
To list famous politicians

explain.en: The book ends with William's quote that any kid can make a difference if they want to, showing the author wants readers to act.
explain.zh: 书末引用威廉的话：任何孩子只要愿意都能带来改变，说明作者希望读者行动起来。

q.en: What 'Don't Waste Food' fact does the book share?
q.zh: 书中“不要浪费食物”部分提到了什么事实？

options.en: Almost no food is wasted
About 25 percent of U.S. food is thrown away according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 美国约25%的食物被丢弃（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: About 40 percent of U.S. food is thrown away
About 10 percent of U.S. food is thrown away

explain.en: The book says about 40 percent of food in the United States is thrown away, wasting money and calories.
explain.zh: 书中说美国约40%的食物被丢弃，既浪费钱也浪费热量。

q.en: What action does the book urge readers to take?
q.zh: 本书敦促读者采取什么行动？

options.en: Ignore the problem
Stop eating vegetables according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 不再吃蔬菜（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Write letters to Congress and help local food banks
options.zh: 给国会写信并帮助当地食物银行

options.en: Move to a farm

explain.en: The book suggests donating, writing to leaders, and learning what local charities need so no child goes hungry.
explain.zh: 书中建议捐赠、给领导人写信，并了解当地慈善机构的需求，让每个孩子都不挨饿。

prompt.en: The book says any kid can make a difference. What is one concrete plan you could start at your school or in your neighborhood to help fight hunger, and who would you ask to help?
prompt.zh: 书里说每个孩子都能带来改变。你能在学校或社区发起的一项具体计划是什么？你会请谁一起帮忙？

en: Think about resources you already have, like a garden, allowance, or a school event.
Start small and name a real first step, such as a food-drive bake sale or a letter to a leader.
zh: 想想你已有的资源，比如菜园、零花钱或学校活动。
从小处着手，定一个真正的第一步，比如义卖或给负责人写信。
